calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-07-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Terrence Mayo and Mary Mayo for Salita Mata.

Mr. Mayo explained that the money from Escrow got put into the Attorney Trust account at the insistence of the Estate Administrator and that she gave instruction to the Title Company without telling them. The Court granted the Petition for Final Distribution, etc. (subject to review of the order).

Judge: John K. Stewart. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Closing Estate
Off Calendar Feb-21-2017 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-06-2017 Continued To Feb-27-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-09-2017 Continued To Feb-06-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-19-2016 Continued To Jan-09-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mary Mayo & Terence Mayo for (administrator/petitioner) Salita Mata.

The Court stated that there are responsive pleadings outstanding. Ms. Mayo stated that the petition was filed quite a while ago, that they would like to distribute by the end of the year, that they addressed the first set of notes, that it was followed by a second set of notes, that there is only one issue left and that the examiner requested they appear regarding fees. The Court stated that they need to address the examiner's notes. Ms. Mayo responded that they are here regarding extraordinary fees. The Court stated that they need to file a declaration. Ms. Mayo stated that they filed a declaration and are now at a loss as to what is wanted. The Court listed the issues pending (escrow, assets on hand & attorney's fees) and that an amended petition is needed. Ms. Mayo stated that the recent declaration addressed the allocation of fees to Mr. Simon and that the tangible personal property is negligible and being distributed to the administrator and beneficiary. Mr. Mayo stated that they have done declarations, that the administrator is out of state and has disabilities, that they have had a hard time getting things done, that the matter keeps getting continued and that they need to address the merits of the petition. The Court stated that it does not see how counsel has addressed the issues if the examiner says they have not been addressed and continued the Petition for Final Distribution to January 9, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for counsel to file a declaration addressing the issues.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)
